- **Autoinflammatory and Immune Dysregulation Syndromes** — Disorders of innate immune regulation causing recurrent inflammation without high-titer autoantibodies.

nodes.name: Immunotherapy and Vaccinology
nodes.en: Covers the deliberate manipulation of immune responses for therapeutic and prophylactic benefit, including vaccine design, cancer immunotherapy, and immunomodulatory biologics. Integrates immunological principles with engineering approaches such as monoclonal antibodies, checkpoint blockade, and cell-based therapies. Represents one of the most translationally active and rapidly evolving frontiers of modern medicine.
nodes.cs: Zahrnuje cílenou manipulaci imunitní odpovědi pro terapeutický a profylaktický prospěch, včetně návrhu vakcín, protinádorové imunoterapie a imunomodulačních biologik. Propojuje imunologické principy s inženýrskými přístupy, jako jsou monoklonální protilátky, blokáda kontrolních bodů a buněčné terapie. Patří k translačně nejaktivnějším a nejrychleji se vyvíjejícím oblastem moderní medicíny.
nodes.brief:
Covers the deliberate manipulation of immune responses for therapeutic and prophylactic benefit, including vaccine design, cancer immunotherapy, and immunomodulatory biologics. Integrates immunological principles with engineering approaches such as monoclonal antibodies, checkpoint blockade, and cell-based therapies. Represents one of the most translationally active and rapidly evolving frontiers of modern medicine.

### Concepts
- **Vaccine Design and Adjuvants** — The rational engineering of antigens and immune-potentiating adjuvants to elicit protective, durable immunity.
- **Cancer Immunotherapy Checkpoint Blockade** — Antibody-based inhibition of PD-1/CTLA-4 and related pathways that restores T cell activity against tumors.
- **CAR-T and Adoptive Cell Therapy** — Engineering of patient-derived T cells with synthetic receptors to redirect cytotoxicity against target antigens.
- **Monoclonal Antibody Therapeutics** — Laboratory-produced antibodies engineered for high specificity to treat cancer, autoimmune disease, and infection.
- **Immune Checkpoint and Cytokine Modulation** — Therapeutic targeting of regulatory pathways and cytokine signaling to fine-tune immune activity in disease.

nodes.name: Immune Signaling and Cytokine Networks
nodes.en: Studies the soluble mediator systems, principally cytokines and chemokines, that coordinate communication between immune cells and orchestrate response magnitude and character. Explains how signaling networks polarize helper T cell responses and drive processes from acute inflammation to chronic disease. Increasingly a frontier for systems-level and computational modeling of immune dynamics.
nodes.cs: Studuje systémy rozpustných mediátorů, především cytokinů a chemokinů, které koordinují komunikaci mezi imunitními buňkami a řídí velikost i charakter odpovědi. Vysvětluje, jak signální sítě polarizují odpovědi pomocných T lymfocytů a řídí procesy od akutního zánětu po chronické onemocnění. Stále více se stává oblastí systémového a výpočetního modelování imunitní dynamiky.
nodes.brief:
Studies the soluble mediator systems, principally cytokines and chemokines, that coordinate communication between immune cells and orchestrate response magnitude and character. Explains how signaling networks polarize helper T cell responses and drive processes from acute inflammation to chronic disease. Increasingly a frontier for systems-level and computational modeling of immune dynamics.

### Concepts
- **Cytokine Signaling Pathways** — JAK-STAT and related intracellular cascades that transmit extracellular cytokine signals to gene expression programs.
- **Chemokines and Leukocyte Trafficking** — Chemotactic protein gradients that direct immune cell migration to sites of infection or lymphoid organs.
- **T Helper Cell Polarization** — Cytokine-driven differentiation of naive CD4+ T cells into Th1, Th2, Th17, or other functionally distinct subsets.
- **Cytokine Storm and Systemic Inflammation** — Dysregulated, self-amplifying cytokine release causing severe systemic tissue damage in infection or therapy.
- **Systems Immunology Modeling** — Computational and high-dimensional approaches that map cytokine and cellular networks to predict immune system behavior.

nodes.name: Microbiology & Virology
nodes.en: Microbiology and virology cover the biology of microorganisms and viruses, from bacterial and archaeal cell physiology and genetics to the ecology of host-associated microbial communities, viral replication strategies, host-pathogen interactions, and the antimicrobial agents and resistance mechanisms that shape infection and treatment.
nodes.cs: Mikrobiologie a virologie se zabývají biologií mikroorganismů a virů - od fyziologie a genetiky bakteriálních a archeálních buněk přes ekologii mikrobiálních společenstev spojených s hostitelem, replikační strategie virů a interakce hostitel-patogen až po antimikrobiální látky a mechanismy rezistence, které určují průběh infekce a léčby.

nodes.name: Bacterial Cell Biology & Physiology
nodes.en: Studies the architecture, growth, and physiological regulation of bacterial and archaeal cells: envelope structure, cell division, sporulation, and metabolic adaptation to environmental stress. Underpins the mechanistic basis of antibiotic targets and stress-survival strategies exploited in biotechnology and infection.
nodes.cs: Studuje architekturu, růst a fyziologickou regulaci bakteriálních a archeálních buněk: strukturu obalu buňky, buněčné dělení, sporulaci a metabolickou adaptaci na stresové podmínky prostředí. Tvoří mechanistický základ pro cíle antibiotik a strategie přežití ve stresu využívané v biotechnologii a při infekci.
nodes.brief:
Studies the architecture, growth, and physiological regulation of bacterial and archaeal cells: envelope structure, cell division, sporulation, and metabolic adaptation to environmental stress. Underpins the mechanistic basis of antibiotic targets and stress-survival strategies exploited in biotechnology and infection.

### Concepts
- **Cell Envelope Architecture** — The peptidoglycan wall, outer membrane, and capsule together determine Gram staining, permeability, and antibiotic susceptibility.
- **Bacterial Cell Cycle & Division** — FtsZ-driven septation and chromosome segregation coordinate binary fission with cell growth.
- **Sporulation & Dormancy** — Endospore formation lets organisms like Bacillus and Clostridium survive extreme desiccation, heat, and starvation for decades.
- **Quorum Sensing** — Cell-density-dependent signaling molecules coordinate collective behaviors such as biofilm formation and virulence-factor expression.
- **Biofilm Formation** — Surface-attached microbial communities encased in self-produced matrix confer antibiotic tolerance and chronic-infection persistence.

nodes.name: Microbial Genetics & Molecular Biology
nodes.en: Examines how bacteria and archaea store, replicate, repair, and horizontally exchange genetic information, and how gene expression is regulated in response to environmental cues. Provides the toolkit and conceptual basis for synthetic biology, CRISPR technology, and molecular epidemiology.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá, jak bakterie a archea uchovávají, replikují, opravují a horizontálně předávají genetickou informaci a jak je genová exprese regulována v reakci na podněty z prostředí. Poskytuje nástroje i pojmový základ pro syntetickou biologii, technologii CRISPR a molekulární epidemiologii.
nodes.brief:
Examines how bacteria and archaea store, replicate, repair, and horizontally exchange genetic information, and how gene expression is regulated in response to environmental cues. Provides the toolkit and conceptual basis for synthetic biology, CRISPR technology, and molecular epidemiology.

### Concepts
- **Horizontal Gene Transfer** — Conjugation, transformation, and transduction move genetic material between cells independent of vertical inheritance.
- **Plasmids & Mobile Genetic Elements** — Self-replicating extrachromosomal DNA and transposons carry accessory genes such as resistance and virulence determinants.
- **Bacterial Gene Regulation** — Operons, sigma factors, and two-component systems let cells rapidly rewire transcription in response to nutrient and stress signals.
- **CRISPR-Cas Systems** — Adaptive prokaryotic immune systems that record and cleave invading phage or plasmid DNA, repurposed as a genome-editing platform.
- **Comparative & Functional Genomics** — Whole-genome sequencing and pan-genome analysis reveal core versus accessory gene content across bacterial populations.

nodes.name: Virology & Virus Replication Strategies
nodes.en: Covers virus structure, classification, and the diverse molecular strategies by which DNA, RNA, and retroviruses enter cells, replicate their genomes, and assemble progeny. Central to understanding emerging viral disease, vaccine design, and antiviral drug development.
nodes.cs: Zahrnuje strukturu a klasifikaci virů a rozmanité molekulární strategie, jimiž DNA viry, RNA viry a retroviry vstupují do buněk, replikují svůj genom a skládají potomstvo. Klíčové pro pochopení nově vznikajících virových onemocnění, návrh vakcín a vývoj antivirotik.
nodes.brief:
Covers virus structure, classification, and the diverse molecular strategies by which DNA, RNA, and retroviruses enter cells, replicate their genomes, and assemble progeny. Central to understanding emerging viral disease, vaccine design, and antiviral drug development.

### Concepts
- **Virus Structure & Classification** — Capsid symmetry, envelope status, and genome type (Baltimore classification) organize viral diversity into replication-strategy groups.
- **Viral Replication Cycles** — Attachment, entry, genome replication, assembly, and egress define the stepwise process by which viruses hijack host machinery.
- **Bacteriophage Biology** — Viruses infecting bacteria drive lytic and lysogenic cycles that shape microbial population dynamics and gene transfer.
- **Viral Genome Evolution** — High mutation and recombination rates, especially in RNA viruses, drive rapid antigenic drift, shift, and quasispecies formation.
- **Emerging & Zoonotic Viruses** — Cross-species spillover events, tracked through phylogenetics and surveillance, underlie pandemic-potential outbreaks.

nodes.name: Microbial Ecology & the Microbiome
nodes.en: Studies microbial communities in natural and host-associated environments — their diversity, interactions, biogeochemical roles, and the assembly rules governing community structure. A rapidly expanding field reshaping views of nutrient cycling, host physiology, and ecosystem function.
nodes.cs: Studuje mikrobiální společenstva v přírodních a s hostitelem spojených prostředích - jejich rozmanitost, interakce, biogeochemické role a pravidla utváření struktury společenstva. Rychle se rozvíjející obor, který mění pohled na koloběh živin, fyziologii hostitele a fungování ekosystémů.
nodes.brief:
Studies microbial communities in natural and host-associated environments — their diversity, interactions, biogeochemical roles, and the assembly rules governing community structure. A rapidly expanding field reshaping views of nutrient cycling, host physiology, and ecosystem function.

### Concepts
- **Human Microbiome** — Resident bacterial, fungal, and viral communities of the gut, skin, and mucosa modulate host immunity, metabolism, and disease risk.
- **Biogeochemical Cycling** — Microbial metabolism drives global carbon, nitrogen, and sulfur cycles through processes like nitrogen fixation and methanogenesis.
- **Metagenomics & Community Profiling** — Culture-independent sequencing of environmental DNA reveals taxonomic and functional composition of complex microbial communities.
- **Microbial Symbiosis** — Mutualistic, commensal, and parasitic relationships between microbes and macro-organisms structure ecological niches across the biosphere.
- **Environmental Microbiology** — Microbial adaptation and function across soil, aquatic, and extreme habitats governs ecosystem-level nutrient and pollutant turnover.

nodes.name: Host-Pathogen Interactions & Pathogenesis
nodes.en: Investigates the molecular and cellular mechanisms by which microbes and viruses cause disease, and the immune strategies hosts deploy to detect and clear infection. Bridges microbiology and immunology to explain virulence, immune evasion, and the outcome of infection.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá molekulární a buněčné mechanismy, jimiž mikroby a viry vyvolávají onemocnění, a imunitní strategie, které hostitel nasazuje k detekci a odstranění infekce. Propojuje mikrobiologii a imunologii při vysvětlení virulence, úniku před imunitním systémem a výsledku infekce.
nodes.brief:
Investigates the molecular and cellular mechanisms by which microbes and viruses cause disease, and the immune strategies hosts deploy to detect and clear infection. Bridges microbiology and immunology to explain virulence, immune evasion, and the outcome of infection.

### Concepts
- **Virulence Factors & Toxins** — Adhesins, secretion systems, and exotoxins/endotoxins enable colonization, tissue damage, and immune subversion by pathogens.
- **Innate Immune Recognition** — Pattern-recognition receptors detect conserved microbial and viral molecular signatures to trigger rapid antimicrobial responses.
- **Immune Evasion Strategies** — Antigenic variation, latency, and effector-protein interference let pathogens escape or suppress host immune clearance.
- **Molecular Koch's Postulates** — Genetic criteria linking specific microbial genes to disease causation guide experimental confirmation of virulence determinants.
- **Infection Dynamics & Epidemiology** — Transmission mode, reproduction number, and within-host kinetics together determine how infections spread through populations.

nodes.name: Antimicrobials, Chemotherapy & Resistance
nodes.en: Covers the mechanisms of action of antibacterial, antiviral, and antifungal agents, and the evolutionary and molecular basis of resistance to them. A field of urgent applied importance given the accelerating global spread of multidrug-resistant organisms.
nodes.cs: Zabývá se mechanismy účinku antibakteriálních, antivirových a antimykotických látek a evolučním a molekulárním základem rezistence vůči nim. Jde o obor naléhavého praktického významu vzhledem ke zrychlujícímu se celosvětovému šíření multirezistentních organismů.
nodes.brief:
Covers the mechanisms of action of antibacterial, antiviral, and antifungal agents, and the evolutionary and molecular basis of resistance to them. A field of urgent applied importance given the accelerating global spread of multidrug-resistant organisms.

### Concepts
- **Antibiotic Mechanisms of Action** — Cell-wall synthesis inhibitors, protein-synthesis blockers, and DNA-replication inhibitors each exploit a distinct bacterial vulnerability.
- **Antimicrobial Resistance Mechanisms** — Target modification, drug efflux, enzymatic inactivation, and reduced permeability confer resistance, often via mobile genetic elements.
- **Antiviral Drug Design** — Polymerase inhibitors, protease inhibitors, and entry blockers target virus-specific enzymes and replication steps.
- **Vaccine Immunology** — Live-attenuated, subunit, and mRNA vaccine platforms elicit protective adaptive immunity against specific pathogens.
- **Phage Therapy & Alternatives** — Bacteriophages and other non-antibiotic strategies are being revisited as tools against multidrug-resistant bacterial infection.

nodes.name: Microbial Diversity, Taxonomy & Extremophiles
nodes.en: Classifies the vast phylogenetic breadth of bacteria, archaea, and viruses, and characterizes organisms adapted to physicochemical extremes. Establishes the systematic framework underlying all applied and ecological microbiology, and probes the outer limits of life's tolerance.
nodes.cs: Klasifikuje širokou fylogenetickou rozmanitost bakterií, archeí a virů a charakterizuje organismy přizpůsobené fyzikálně-chemickým extrémům. Ustavuje systematický rámec, na němž stojí veškerá aplikovaná a ekologická mikrobiologie, a zkoumá krajní meze tolerance života.
nodes.brief:
Classifies the vast phylogenetic breadth of bacteria, archaea, and viruses, and characterizes organisms adapted to physicochemical extremes. Establishes the systematic framework underlying all applied and ecological microbiology, and probes the outer limits of life's tolerance.

### Concepts
- **Prokaryotic Phylogeny** — 16S/18S rRNA and whole-genome phylogenetics reconstruct evolutionary relationships across the bacterial and archaeal domains.
- **Archaea Biology** — A distinct domain of prokaryotes with unique membrane lipids and transcription machinery, often thriving in extreme environments.
- **Extremophiles** — Organisms adapted to extremes of temperature, pH, salinity, or pressure reveal the biochemical limits of cellular life.
- **Fungal & Protist Microbiology** — Eukaryotic microbes including yeasts, molds, and protozoa contribute distinct pathogenic, ecological, and industrial roles.
- **Culturing & Isolation Techniques** — Selective media, enrichment culture, and single-cell isolation methods recover and characterize novel microbial taxa.

nodes.name: Structural Biology
nodes.en: Structural biology determines the three-dimensional architecture of proteins, nucleic acids, and macromolecular assemblies, and relates that architecture to biological function. It integrates experimental methods — X-ray crystallography, cryo-electron microscopy, and NMR spectroscopy — with computational structure prediction to resolve folding, conformational dynamics, and assembly at atomic resolution. It differs from Molecular Biology in its focus on physical structure rather than gene expression and regulatory mechanism.
nodes.cs: Strukturní biologie určuje trojrozměrnou architekturu proteinů, nukleových kyselin a makromolekulárních komplexů a dává tuto architekturu do souvislosti s biologickou funkcí. Kombinuje experimentální metody – rentgenovou krystalografii, kryoelektronovou mikroskopii a NMR spektroskopii – s výpočetní predikcí struktury, aby popsala skládání, konformační dynamiku a sestavování na atomární úrovni. Na rozdíl od molekulární biologie se zaměřuje na fyzickou strukturu, nikoli na genovou expresi a regulační mechanismy.

nodes.name: Protein Folding & Stability
nodes.en: Studies how a linear polypeptide chain reaches its native three-dimensional conformation, the thermodynamic and kinetic principles governing that process, and the pathologies that arise when folding fails. Anfinsen's thermodynamic hypothesis and the energy-landscape (funnel) theory underpin the field's core explanatory framework. Chaperone-assisted folding and aggregation/misfolding diseases connect the theory directly to cell biology and medicine.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá, jak lineární polypeptidový řetězec dosahuje své nativní trojrozměrné konformace, jaké termodynamické a kinetické principy tento proces řídí a jaké patologie vznikají při selhání skládání. Jádrem výkladového rámce oboru je Anfinsenova termodynamická hypotéza a teorie energetické krajiny (trychtýře). Skládání za asistence chaperonů a nemoci spojené s agregací a nesprávným skládáním propojují tuto teorii přímo s buněčnou biologií a medicínou.
nodes.brief:
Studies how a linear polypeptide chain reaches its native three-dimensional conformation, the thermodynamic and kinetic principles governing that process, and the pathologies that arise when folding fails. Anfinsen's thermodynamic hypothesis and the energy-landscape (funnel) theory underpin the field's core explanatory framework. Chaperone-assisted folding and aggregation/misfolding diseases connect the theory directly to cell biology and medicine.

### Concepts
- **Anfinsen's Dogma** — A protein's native structure is determined solely by its amino-acid sequence under physiological conditions.
- **Folding Funnel / Energy Landscape** — A statistical-mechanical model in which folding proceeds down a funnel-shaped free-energy surface toward the native minimum.
- **Molecular Chaperones** — Proteins such as Hsp70 and GroEL/GroES that assist correct folding and prevent aggregation of nascent or stressed polypeptides.
- **Protein Misfolding & Amyloidosis** — Aberrant folding pathways that produce cross-beta amyloid fibrils implicated in diseases like Alzheimer's and prion disorders.
- **Kinetics of Folding** — Experimental and theoretical analysis of folding rates, intermediates, and transition states via methods like Phi-value analysis.

nodes.name: X-Ray Crystallography
nodes.en: The longest-established technique for atomic-resolution structure determination, using diffraction of X-rays by ordered crystal lattices of macromolecules to reconstruct electron density maps. Covers crystallization strategies, the phase problem and its solutions, and refinement of atomic models against diffraction data. Remains the dominant source of high-resolution structures in the PDB and the benchmark against which newer methods are validated.
nodes.cs: Nejdéle zavedená technika stanovení struktury s atomárním rozlišením, využívající difrakci rentgenového záření na uspořádaných krystalových mřížkách makromolekul k rekonstrukci map elektronové hustoty. Zahrnuje strategie krystalizace, fázový problém a jeho řešení a zpřesňování atomárních modelů podle difrakčních dat. Zůstává dominantním zdrojem struktur s vysokým rozlišením v databázi PDB a měřítkem, vůči němuž se validují novější metody.
nodes.brief:
The longest-established technique for atomic-resolution structure determination, using diffraction of X-rays by ordered crystal lattices of macromolecules to reconstruct electron density maps. Covers crystallization strategies, the phase problem and its solutions, and refinement of atomic models against diffraction data. Remains the dominant source of high-resolution structures in the PDB and the benchmark against which newer methods are validated.

### Concepts
- **Protein Crystallization** — Empirical and rational methods for growing ordered macromolecular crystals suitable for diffraction, typically via vapor diffusion or lipidic cubic phase.
- **The Phase Problem** — The loss of phase information in diffraction data, solved via molecular replacement, isomorphous replacement, or anomalous dispersion (MAD/SAD).
- **Synchrotron Diffraction** — Use of high-brilliance synchrotron X-ray sources to collect diffraction data from small or weakly diffracting crystals.
- **Structure Refinement** — Iterative fitting of an atomic model against observed structure factors to minimize R-factors and produce a validated electron-density-consistent structure.
- **Serial Femtosecond Crystallography** — Use of X-ray free-electron lasers to collect diffraction from micro/nanocrystals before radiation damage destroys them, enabling room-temperature and time-resolved studies.

nodes.name: Cryo-Electron Microscopy
nodes.en: Determines macromolecular structures by imaging vitrified, unstained specimens with an electron microscope and computationally reconstructing three-dimensional density from thousands of single-particle projections. The resolution revolution driven by direct electron detectors and improved image-processing algorithms has made cryo-EM the leading method for large complexes and membrane proteins resistant to crystallization. Cryo-electron tomography extends the approach to structures in situ within intact cells.
nodes.cs: Určuje strukturu makromolekul zobrazováním vitrifikovaných, nebarvených vzorků elektronovým mikroskopem a výpočetní rekonstrukcí trojrozměrné hustoty z tisíců projekcí jednotlivých částic. Revoluce v rozlišení, kterou přinesly přímé elektronové detektory a zdokonalené algoritmy zpracování obrazu, učinila kryoelektronovou mikroskopii vedoucí metodou pro velké komplexy a membránové proteiny odolné vůči krystalizaci. Kryoelektronová tomografie rozšiřuje tento přístup na struktury in situ uvnitř neporušených buněk.
nodes.brief:
Determines macromolecular structures by imaging vitrified, unstained specimens with an electron microscope and computationally reconstructing three-dimensional density from thousands of single-particle projections. The 'resolution revolution' driven by direct electron detectors and improved image-processing algorithms has made cryo-EM the leading method for large complexes and membrane proteins resistant to crystallization. Cryo-electron tomography extends the approach to str

### Concepts
- **Single-Particle Analysis** — Computational averaging and 3D reconstruction of thousands of randomly oriented particle images to yield a near-atomic-resolution density map.
- **Vitrification** — Rapid plunge-freezing of specimens in a thin layer of amorphous ice to preserve native structure without crystalline-ice damage.
- **Direct Electron Detectors** — Detector technology enabling motion-correction and dose-fractionation that drove the resolution revolution of the 2010s.
- **Cryo-Electron Tomography** — Tilt-series imaging and subtomogram averaging used to resolve macromolecular structures in their native cellular context.
- **Map-to-Model Building** — Fitting and refining atomic coordinates into cryo-EM density maps, often combined with predicted models.

nodes.name: NMR Spectroscopy of Macromolecules
nodes.en: Uses nuclear magnetic resonance to determine solution-state structures and, uniquely among the major methods, to directly probe conformational dynamics of proteins and nucleic acids at atomic resolution. Multidimensional heteronuclear experiments and isotope labeling extract distance and angle restraints that are converted into structural ensembles rather than single static models. Complements crystallography and cryo-EM by capturing motion, transient states, and disorder in solution, without requiring crystallization or vitrification.
nodes.cs: Využívá nukleární magnetickou rezonanci ke stanovení struktur v roztoku a jako jediná z hlavních metod umožňuje přímo sledovat konformační dynamiku proteinů a nukleových kyselin s atomárním rozlišením. Vícerozměrné heteronukleární experimenty a izotopové značení poskytují vzdálenostní a úhlová omezení, která se převádějí na soubory struktur místo jediného statického modelu. Doplňuje krystalografii a kryoelektronovou mikroskopii tím, že zachycuje pohyb, přechodné stavy a neuspořádanost v roztoku, aniž by vyžadovala krystalizaci nebo vitrifikaci.
nodes.brief:
Uses nuclear magnetic resonance to determine solution-state structures and, uniquely among the major methods, to directly probe conformational dynamics of proteins and nucleic acids at atomic resolution. Multidimensional heteronuclear experiments and isotope labeling extract distance and angle restraints that are converted into structural ensembles rather than single static models. Complements crystallography and cryo-EM by capturing motion, transient states, and disorder in 

### Concepts
- **Multidimensional Heteronuclear NMR** — 2D/3D/4D experiments (e.g. HSQC, NOESY) exploiting 1H, 13C, 15N labeling to resolve overlapping resonances and assign spectra.
- **NOE Distance Restraints** — Nuclear Overhauser effect cross-peaks reporting through-space proton-proton distances used to compute 3D structures.
- **Relaxation Dispersion & Dynamics** — NMR relaxation methods that quantify protein motions spanning picoseconds to milliseconds, including sparsely populated excited states.
- **Intrinsically Disordered Proteins by NMR** — Application of NMR chemical-shift and relaxation data to characterize proteins lacking a stable folded structure.
- **Solid-State NMR** — NMR methods applied to non-soluble samples such as membrane proteins in lipid bilayers and amyloid fibrils.

nodes.name: Computational Structure Prediction & Modeling
nodes.en: Encompasses physics-based and, increasingly, deep-learning approaches that predict three-dimensional structure directly from amino-acid sequence, bypassing experimental determination. AlphaFold2 and successor models achieved near-experimental accuracy on the CASP benchmark and reframed structural biology as partly a computational discipline. Includes homology modeling, molecular dynamics simulation, and de novo protein design as computational complements to X-ray, cryo-EM, and NMR determination.
nodes.cs: Zahrnuje fyzikálně založené a stále více i hlubokým učením řízené přístupy, které predikují trojrozměrnou strukturu přímo z aminokyselinové sekvence bez nutnosti experimentálního určení. AlphaFold2 a jeho nástupci dosáhli v benchmarku CASP téměř experimentální přesnosti a proměnily strukturní biologii částečně ve výpočetní obor. Zahrnuje homologní modelování, simulace molekulární dynamiky a de novo návrh proteinů jako výpočetní doplněk k rentgenové krystalografii, kryo-elektronové mikroskopii a NMR.
nodes.brief:
Encompasses physics-based and, increasingly, deep-learning approaches that predict three-dimensional structure directly from amino-acid sequence, bypassing experimental determination. The field was transformed by AlphaFold2 and successor models, which achieved near-experimental accuracy on the CASP benchmark and reframed structural biology as partly a computational discipline. Includes homology modeling, molecular dynamics simulation, and protein design as complementary compu

### Concepts
- **Deep-Learning Structure Prediction** — Neural network systems such as AlphaFold and RoseTTAFold that predict atomic structure from sequence using coevolutionary and geometric deep learning.
- **CASP Benchmark** — The biennial Critical Assessment of protein Structure Prediction competition that blind-tests predictive methods against unpublished experimental structures.
- **Homology (Comparative) Modeling** — Building a structural model of a target sequence using a homologous solved structure as a template.
- **Molecular Dynamics Simulation** — Numerical integration of atomic forces over time to simulate conformational motion and energetics of macromolecules.
- **De Novo Protein Design** — Computational generation of novel protein sequences and folds not found in nature, using physics-based or generative-AI methods.

nodes.name: Structural Genomics & Macromolecular Assemblies
nodes.en: Addresses structure determination and architectural principles of large multi-subunit machines such as ribosomes, spliceosomes, viral capsids, and nuclear pore complexes that carry out coordinated biological functions beyond any single polypeptide. Structural genomics initiatives systematically determined representative folds across protein families to map fold space. Integrative/hybrid modeling combines multiple experimental data types to resolve assemblies too large or heterogeneous for any single technique alone.
nodes.cs: Zabývá se určováním struktury a architektonickými principy velkých mnohapodjednotkových komplexů, jako jsou ribozomy, spliceozomy, virové kapsidy a komplexy jaderných pórů, které plní koordinované biologické funkce přesahující rámec jediného polypeptidu. Iniciativy strukturní genomiky systematicky určily reprezentativní strukturní motivy napříč proteinovými rodinami a zmapovaly tak prostor možných struktur. Integrativní/hybridní modelování kombinuje více typů experimentálních dat k řešení komplexů příliš velkých nebo heterogenních pro jedinou samostatnou techniku.
nodes.brief:
Addresses structure determination and architectural principles of large multi-subunit machines such as ribosomes, spliceosomes, viral capsids, and nuclear pore complexes that carry out coordinated biological functions beyond any single polypeptide. Structural genomics initiatives systematically determined representative folds across protein families to map fold space. Integrative/hybrid modeling combines multiple experimental data types to resolve assemblies too large or hete

### Concepts
- **Ribosome Structure** — Atomic-resolution architecture of the ribosomal RNA-protein machine that catalyzes mRNA translation, resolved by crystallography and cryo-EM.
- **Viral Capsid Architecture** — Study of icosahedral and helical symmetry principles governing self-assembly of protein shells that encapsidate viral genomes.
- **Integrative/Hybrid Structural Modeling** — Combining crystallography, cryo-EM, cross-linking mass spectrometry, and SAXS data to model assemblies inaccessible to any single technique.
- **Structural Genomics Initiatives** — Large-scale, high-throughput programs that solved representative structures across protein fold families to comprehensively map fold space.
- **Protein Fold Classification** — Hierarchical taxonomies such as SCOP and CATH that organize known structures by evolutionary and structural relationship.

nodes.name: Membrane Protein Structural Biology
nodes.en: Focuses on the distinct experimental and biophysical challenges of determining structures of proteins embedded in lipid bilayers, such as receptors, channels, and transporters, which historically lagged soluble proteins due to instability outside a native membrane environment. Specialized methods including lipidic cubic phase crystallization, nanodiscs, and detergent micelles were developed specifically to stabilize these targets. GPCR and ion-channel structures resolved by these methods underpin a large share of modern structure-based drug design.
nodes.cs: Zaměřuje se na specifické experimentální a biofyzikální problémy spojené s určováním struktury proteinů zanořených do lipidové dvojvrstvy, jako jsou receptory, kanály a transportéry, které historicky zaostávaly za rozpustnými proteiny kvůli nestabilitě mimo nativní membránové prostředí. Pro stabilizaci těchto cílů byly vyvinuty specializované metody, včetně krystalizace v lipidové kubické fázi, nanodisků a detergentových micel. Struktury GPCR a iontových kanálů určené těmito metodami tvoří velkou část moderního strukturně cíleného návrhu léčiv.
nodes.brief:
Focuses on the distinct experimental and biophysical challenges of determining structures of proteins embedded in lipid bilayers, such as receptors, channels, and transporters, which historically lagged soluble proteins due to instability outside a native membrane environment. Specialized methods including lipidic cubic phase crystallization, nanodiscs, and detergent micelles were developed specifically to stabilize these targets. GPCR and ion-channel structures resolved by t

### Concepts
- **Lipidic Cubic Phase Crystallization** — A membrane-mimetic crystallization matrix that enabled high-resolution structures of GPCRs and other integral membrane proteins.
- **Nanodiscs & Detergent Micelles** — Membrane-mimetic systems that solubilize and stabilize membrane proteins outside the cell while preserving native lipid contacts.
- **GPCR Structural Biology** — Determination of G-protein-coupled receptor conformations, including active and inactive states, foundational to modern pharmacology.
- **Ion Channel Structure & Gating** — Structural characterization of the conformational states underlying ion channel opening, closing, and selectivity.
- **Cryo-EM for Membrane Proteins** — Application of single-particle cryo-EM, which avoids crystallization entirely, to resolve membrane protein structures at near-atomic resolution.

nodes.name: Structure-Function Relationships & Conformational Dynamics
nodes.en: Connects static atomic structures to biological mechanism, examining how conformational change, allostery, and molecular motion enable catalysis, signaling, and macromolecular recognition. Time-resolved and single-molecule biophysical methods extend beyond static snapshots to capture structural transitions directly. This block is the interpretive bridge that gives structural data mechanistic and functional meaning for enzymology, pharmacology, and molecular biology.
nodes.cs: Propojuje statické atomové struktury s biologickým mechanismem a zkoumá, jak konformační změny, alosterie a molekulární pohyb umožňují katalýzu, signalizaci a molekulární rozpoznávání. Časově rozlišené a jednomolekulové biofyzikální metody jdou nad rámec statických snímků a zachycují strukturní přechody přímo. Tento úsek je interpretačním mostem, který dává strukturním datům mechanistický a funkční význam pro enzymologii, farmakologii a molekulární biologii.
nodes.brief:
Connects static atomic structures to biological mechanism, examining how conformational change, allostery, and molecular motion enable catalysis, signaling, and macromolecular recognition. Time-resolved and single-molecule biophysical methods extend beyond static snapshots to capture structural transitions directly. This block is the interpretive bridge that gives structural data mechanistic and functional meaning for enzymology, pharmacology, and molecular biology.

### Concepts
- **Allostery** — Regulation of a macromolecule's function at one site through conformational changes propagated from a distal ligand-binding site.
- **Enzyme Active-Site Geometry** — Structural analysis of catalytic residue arrangement and substrate binding that explains reaction mechanism and specificity.
- **Time-Resolved Structural Methods** — Pump-probe crystallography and XFEL techniques that capture structural intermediates along a reaction coordinate in real time.
- **Single-Molecule Biophysics** — FRET and force-spectroscopy techniques that observe conformational dynamics and folding of individual macromolecules directly.
- **Structure-Based Drug Design** — Use of experimentally or computationally determined target structures to rationally design and optimize small-molecule therapeutics.

nodes.name: Systems & Synthetic Biology
nodes.en: Studies and engineers biological systems using quantitative, network-level models and applies that understanding to build new biological function. Spans systems-biology theory and network modeling, synthetic gene circuits, metabolic engineering, genome editing, and design-build-test-learn biofoundry workflows that turn biology into an engineering discipline.
nodes.cs: Studuje a inženýrsky navrhuje biologické systémy pomocí kvantitativních, síťově pojatých modelů a toto porozumění využívá k vytváření nové biologické funkce. Zahrnuje teorii systémové biologie a modelování sítí, syntetické genové obvody, metabolické inženýrství, editaci genomu a workflow navrhni-postav-otestuj-uč se v biofoundry, které proměňují biologii v inženýrskou disciplínu.

nodes.name: Systems Biology Theory & Network Modeling
nodes.en: Studies the quantitative, dynamical structure of biological networks — how gene, protein, and metabolic interactions generate emergent cell behavior. Provides the mathematical vocabulary (ODEs, stochastic processes, Boolean logic, control theory) that underlies all engineered biological design. Largely mature: core formalisms date to the 2000s systems-biology wave and are textbook-standard.
nodes.cs: Studuje kvantitativní, dynamickou strukturu biologických sítí — jak interakce genů, proteinů a metabolických drah generují emergentní chování buňky. Poskytuje matematický aparát (obyčejné diferenciální rovnice, stochastické procesy, booleovskou logiku, teorii řízení), který je základem veškerého inženýrského návrhu biologických systémů. Jde z velké části o zavedenou oblast: základní formalismy pocházejí z vlny systémové biologie 2000. let a jsou učebnicovým standardem.
nodes.brief:
Studies the quantitative, dynamical structure of biological networks — how gene, protein, and metabolic interactions generate emergent cell behavior. Provides the mathematical vocabulary (ODEs, stochastic processes, Boolean logic, control theory) that underlies all engineered biological design. Largely mature: core formalisms date to the 2000s systems-biology wave and are textbook-standard.

### Concepts
- **Boolean & Logical Network Models** — Represent gene states as discrete on/off nodes updated by logical rules to capture regulatory dynamics qualitatively.
- **ODE Kinetic Modeling** — Uses coupled ordinary differential equations to describe continuous concentration changes of interacting molecular species over time.
- **Stochastic Gene Expression Modeling** — Applies the chemical master equation and Gillespie simulation to capture noise from low-copy-number molecular events.
- **Control Theory & Feedback Analysis** — Analyzes robustness, feedback loops, and homeostasis in cellular circuits using engineering control-systems frameworks.
- **Network Motifs & Topology** — Identifies recurring regulatory wiring patterns, such as feedforward loops, that recur across organisms and confer specific dynamic functions.
- **Multi-Omics Data Integration** — Combines transcriptomic, proteomic, and metabolomic datasets into unified computational models of cell state.

nodes.name: Synthetic Gene Circuit Engineering
nodes.en: Designs and builds artificial regulatory networks — switches, oscillators, logic gates, and sensors — from standardized genetic parts inserted into living cells. Founded by landmark repressilator and toggle-switch circuits in 2000, the field now engineers increasingly reliable, composable, and predictable cellular computation. Remains an active frontier for scaling circuit complexity and portability across chassis.
nodes.cs: Navrhuje a konstruuje umělé regulační sítě — přepínače, oscilátory, logická hradla a senzory — ze standardizovaných genetických dílů vložených do živých buněk. Obor založily přelomové obvody typu represilátor a přepínač (toggle switch) v roce 2000 a dnes směřuje ke stále spolehlivější, skladatelnější a předvídatelnější buněčné výpočetní technice. Zůstává aktivní hranicí výzkumu při škálování složitosti obvodů a jejich přenositelnosti mezi hostitelskými organismy.
nodes.brief:
Designs and builds artificial regulatory networks — switches, oscillators, logic gates, and sensors — from standardized genetic parts inserted into living cells. Founded by landmark repressilator and toggle-switch circuits in 2000, the field now engineers increasingly reliable, composable, and predictable cellular computation. Remains an active frontier for scaling circuit complexity and portability across chassis.

### Concepts
- **Transcriptional Logic Gates** — Compose promoters and repressors into AND, OR, and NOT gates that perform Boolean computation inside living cells.
- **Toggle Switches & Genetic Oscillators** — Bistable and periodic circuits, exemplified by the toggle switch and repressilator, that create memory and rhythmic gene expression.
- **Synthetic Feedback Controllers** — Engineered feedback loops that regulate circuit output around a set point despite host and environmental perturbation.
- **Riboswitches & RNA-Based Circuits** — RNA elements that sense ligands or interact via base-pairing to control translation or transcription without protein intermediates.
- **Quorum-Sensing & Cell-Cell Signaling Circuits** — Diffusible small-molecule communication systems repurposed to synchronize gene expression across a cell population.
- **Biosensor & Reporter Circuits** — Genetic constructs that couple environmental or intracellular signal detection to a measurable output such as fluorescence.

nodes.name: Metabolic Engineering & Flux Analysis
nodes.en: Redirects and optimizes cellular metabolism to produce target chemicals, fuels, and materials by rewiring enzymatic pathways and their regulation. Draws on genome-scale stoichiometric models and flux measurement to predict and improve strain performance. Core flux-balance methods are well established; dynamic and predictive pathway control remain active engineering challenges.
nodes.cs: Přesměrovává a optimalizuje buněčný metabolismus k produkci cílových chemikálií, paliv a materiálů přestavbou enzymatických drah a jejich regulace. Čerpá z genomově rozsáhlých stechiometrických modelů a měření toků k predikci a zlepšování výkonnosti kmenů. Základní metody bilance toků (flux-balance) jsou dobře zavedené; dynamická a prediktivní kontrola drah zůstává aktivní inženýrskou výzvou.
nodes.brief:
Redirects and optimizes cellular metabolism to produce target chemicals, fuels, and materials by rewiring enzymatic pathways and their regulation. Draws on genome-scale stoichiometric models and flux measurement to predict and improve strain performance. Core flux-balance methods are well established; dynamic and predictive pathway control remain active engineering challenges.

### Concepts
- **Flux Balance Analysis** — A constraint-based optimization method that predicts steady-state metabolic flux distributions from stoichiometry and an objective function.
- **Genome-Scale Metabolic Models** — Comprehensive reconstructions of an organism's full enzymatic reaction network used to simulate and engineer metabolism computationally.
- **Heterologous Pathway Engineering** — Introduces and optimizes multi-enzyme biosynthetic pathways from one organism into a production host chassis.
- **Metabolic Control Analysis** — Quantifies how sensitively pathway flux responds to changes in individual enzyme activities to identify rate-limiting steps.
- **Dynamic Flux Regulation** — Uses inducible or feedback-controlled genetic elements to reroute flux in response to real-time metabolic state.
- **Cofactor & Redox Balancing** — Engineers NAD(P)H and ATP supply-demand ratios to sustain high-yield production of engineered biosynthetic pathways.

nodes.name: Genome Engineering & Editing
nodes.en: Encompasses the tools and strategies for precisely rewriting DNA sequence in living cells and whole genomes, from single-base edits to synthesized chromosomes. CRISPR-Cas systems since 2012 transformed the field from rare, laborious edits to routine, programmable ones. Editor precision, delivery, and whole-genome synthesis remain fast-moving research frontiers.
nodes.brief:
Encompasses the tools and strategies for precisely rewriting DNA sequence in living cells and whole genomes, from single-base edits to synthesized chromosomes. CRISPR-Cas systems since 2012 transformed the field from rare, laborious edits to routine, programmable ones. Editor precision, delivery, and whole-genome synthesis remain fast-moving research frontiers.

### Concepts
- **CRISPR-Cas Systems** — RNA-guided nucleases that generate targeted double-strand breaks or bind DNA for programmable genome modification and regulation.
- **Base Editing & Prime Editing** — Nuclease-free or nick-based editors that install precise nucleotide changes without relying on double-strand-break repair.
- **Genome Writing & Synthesis** — De novo chemical synthesis and assembly of whole chromosomes or genomes, exemplified by recoded synthetic Escherichia coli and yeast.
- **Directed Evolution of Editing Tools** — Iterative mutagenesis and selection that improve nuclease specificity, PAM range, and delivery efficiency of genome editors.
- **Multiplexed Automated Genome Engineering** — Recombineering-based methods, such as MAGE, that introduce many simultaneous mutations across a genome for combinatorial optimization.
- **Epigenome Editing** — Uses catalytically dead nucleases fused to chromatin modifiers to reprogram gene expression without altering DNA sequence.

nodes.name: Design-Build-Test-Learn & Biofoundry Automation
nodes.en: Formalizes synthetic biology as an engineering discipline through the iterative Design-Build-Test-Learn cycle, supported by standardized DNA assembly, robotic biofoundries, and computational design tools. Aims to make strain and circuit engineering as reproducible and scalable as electronic circuit design. An actively developing frontier where automation and machine learning are reshaping throughput.
nodes.brief:
Formalizes synthetic biology as an engineering discipline through the iterative Design-Build-Test-Learn cycle, supported by standardized DNA assembly, robotic biofoundries, and computational design tools. Aims to make strain and circuit engineering as reproducible and scalable as electronic circuit design. An actively developing frontier where automation and machine learning are reshaping throughput.

### Concepts
- **Standardized DNA Assembly** — Modular cloning frameworks, such as Golden Gate and MoClo, that enable predictable, hierarchical combination of genetic parts.
- **Biofoundry Automation** — Robotic, high-throughput platforms that execute construct assembly, transformation, and screening at industrial scale.
- **Machine-Learning-Guided Design** — Uses trained predictive models to select genetic parts, pathway combinations, or regulatory sequences prior to wet-lab testing.
- **Genetic Part Characterization** — Quantifies promoter, RBS, and terminator performance under standardized conditions to build reusable, predictable part libraries.
- **Computer-Aided Genetic Design** — Software tools that model, simulate, and auto-generate DNA sequences for target circuit or pathway function before synthesis.

nodes.name: Cell-Free Systems & Minimal Cell Engineering
nodes.en: Builds and studies biological function outside, or with a radically reduced version of, the living cell — using purified transcription-translation extracts, minimal genomes, and synthetic vesicles. Removes growth and viability constraints, enabling rapid prototyping and probing of life's essential requirements. A frontier area bridging synthetic biology with origin-of-life and bottom-up biophysics research.
nodes.brief:
Builds and studies biological function outside, or with a radically reduced version of, the living cell — using purified transcription-translation extracts, minimal genomes, and synthetic vesicles. Removes growth and viability constraints, enabling rapid prototyping and probing of life's essential requirements. A frontier area bridging synthetic biology with origin-of-life and bottom-up biophysics research.

### Concepts
- **Cell-Free Transcription-Translation Systems** — In vitro extracts or reconstituted systems that execute gene expression outside a living cell for rapid circuit prototyping.
- **Minimal Genome Construction** — Identifies and builds the smallest gene set sufficient to sustain independent cellular life, as in the JCVI-syn3.0 program.
- **Protocell & Synthetic Vesicle Engineering** — Constructs lipid- or polymer-bounded compartments that replicate basic cellular functions such as division and metabolism.
- **In Vitro Biosensing Platforms** — Deploys cell-free circuits in portable, freeze-dried formats for point-of-need diagnostic and environmental sensing.

nodes.name: Multicellular & Chassis Systems Engineering
nodes.en: Extends synthetic biology beyond single circuits to engineered consortia, alternative chassis organisms, and coordinated multicellular behavior such as spatial patterning. Addresses how engineered function scales, evolves stably, and interacts within populations and ecosystems. A rapidly developing frontier central to bioproduction robustness and synthetic developmental biology.
nodes.brief:
Extends synthetic biology beyond single circuits to engineered consortia, alternative chassis organisms, and coordinated multicellular behavior such as spatial patterning. Addresses how engineered function scales, evolves stably, and interacts within populations and ecosystems. A rapidly developing frontier central to bioproduction robustness and synthetic developmental biology.

### Concepts
- **Synthetic Microbial Consortia** — Engineers division of labor and stable coexistence among multiple interacting engineered strains or species.
- **Chassis Organism Engineering** — Adapts non-model or extremophile organisms as production or sensing hosts to exploit traits absent in standard chassis.
- **Spatial Pattern-Formation Circuits** — Synthetic genetic programs that generate reproducible spatial gene-expression patterns, such as synthetic Turing-like systems.
- **Synthetic Multicellularity** — Engineers cell-cell adhesion and signaling programs that drive artificial developmental processes and tissue-like organization.
- **Xenobiology & Orthogonal Biosystems** — Builds genetic systems using non-natural bases, amino acids, or codes that are functionally isolated from native biology for biocontainment.

nodes.name: Biophysics
nodes.en: Biophysics applies the quantitative methods and theoretical frameworks of physics — statistical mechanics, mechanics, and electromagnetism — to explain how molecular motors, membranes, ion channels, and folded proteins generate biological function from physical forces and energetics. It spans single-molecule measurement techniques through nonequilibrium and active-matter theory, distinguishing itself from structural biology by modeling dynamics and forces rather than static molecular architecture.
nodes.cs: Biofyzika aplikuje kvantitativní metody a teoretické rámce fyziky – statistickou mechaniku, mechaniku a elektromagnetismus – na vysvětlení toho, jak molekulární motory, membrány, iontové kanály a sbalené proteiny vytvářejí biologickou funkci z fyzikálních sil a energetiky. Zahrnuje jak metody měření na úrovni jednotlivých molekul, tak teorii nerovnovážných a aktivních soustav, čímž se odlišuje od strukturní biologie důrazem na dynamiku a síly spíše než na statickou molekulární architekturu.

nodes.name: Statistical & Thermodynamic Physics of Biomolecules
nodes.en: Applies equilibrium and near-equilibrium statistical mechanics to macromolecular conformation, binding, and self-assembly. Provides the free-energy, entropy, and fluctuation-dissipation framework underlying every quantitative model of biological structure and function. Forms the theoretical bedrock on which molecular motor, folding, and membrane models are built.
nodes.brief:
Applies equilibrium and near-equilibrium statistical mechanics to macromolecular conformation, binding, and self-assembly. Provides the free-energy, entropy, and fluctuation-dissipation framework underlying every quantitative model of biological structure and function. Forms the theoretical bedrock on which molecular motor, folding, and membrane models are built.

### Concepts
- **Polymer Physics of Biomolecules** — Describes DNA, RNA, and unfolded proteins as worm-like or freely-jointed chains governed by entropic elasticity.
- **Ligand-Binding Thermodynamics** — Uses partition functions and binding polynomials to quantify cooperativity and allostery in receptor-ligand systems.
- **Fluctuation Theorems** — Jarzynski and Crooks relations connect nonequilibrium work distributions to equilibrium free-energy differences.
- **Osmotic and Electrostatic Interactions** — Poisson-Boltzmann and Donnan equilibrium theory quantify charge screening and osmotic pressure around biopolymers.
- **Statistical Mechanics of Self-Assembly** — Predicts micelle, capsid, and fiber formation from pairwise interaction potentials and concentration-dependent equilibria.

nodes.name: Molecular Motors & Cytoskeletal Mechanics
nodes.en: Studies how ATP-hydrolyzing proteins convert chemical free energy into directed mechanical work along cytoskeletal filaments and nucleic acids. Covers force generation, stepping kinetics, and collective behavior of motors such as myosin, kinesin, dynein, and polymerases. Central to understanding muscle contraction, intracellular transport, and cell division.
nodes.brief:
Studies how ATP-hydrolyzing proteins convert chemical free energy into directed mechanical work along cytoskeletal filaments and nucleic acids. Covers force generation, stepping kinetics, and collective behavior of motors such as myosin, kinesin, dynein, and polymerases. Central to understanding muscle contraction, intracellular transport, and cell division.

### Concepts
- **Myosin-Actin Crossbridge Cycle** — Describes the ATPase-coupled power stroke that generates sliding-filament force in muscle and non-muscle myosins.
- **Kinesin and Dynein Stepping** — Characterizes hand-over-hand and inchworm mechanisms of processive transport along microtubules.
- **Brownian Ratchet Models** — Explain directed motor motion as rectified thermal fluctuations biased by chemical-state-dependent potentials.
- **Filament Polymerization Dynamics** — Treats actin and microtubule growth and catastrophe as stochastic, force-sensitive assembly processes.
- **Collective Motor Ensembles** — Models how multiple motors sharing a cargo generate emergent force-velocity and tug-of-war behavior.

nodes.name: Membrane Biophysics
nodes.en: Examines the physical properties of lipid bilayers—curvature, elasticity, phase behavior, and fusion—and how they shape and are shaped by embedded proteins. Underpins mechanisms of vesicle trafficking, endocytosis, and organelle morphology. Bridges continuum elasticity theory with molecular-scale lipid organization.
nodes.brief:
Examines the physical properties of lipid bilayers—curvature, elasticity, phase behavior, and fusion—and how they shape and are shaped by embedded proteins. Underpins mechanisms of vesicle trafficking, endocytosis, and organelle morphology. Bridges continuum elasticity theory with molecular-scale lipid organization.

### Concepts
- **Helfrich Elasticity Theory** — Models bilayer bending energy as a function of curvature, spontaneous curvature, and surface tension.
- **Lipid Phase Behavior** — Describes liquid-ordered/disordered coexistence and raft formation from lipid composition and temperature.
- **Membrane Fusion and Fission** — Characterizes the energetics of hemifusion intermediates driven by SNARE proteins and curvature-inducing coats.
- **Protein-Induced Membrane Deformation** — Explains how BAR-domain and amphipathic-helix proteins sculpt local membrane curvature.
- **Membrane Permeability and Transport** — Quantifies passive diffusion of solutes across bilayers using solubility-diffusion theory.

nodes.name: Ion Channel & Electrophysiology Biophysics
nodes.en: Analyzes the biophysical mechanisms of selective ion permeation, gating, and voltage sensing in membrane channels and pumps. Combines electrodiffusion theory, structural biology, and patch-clamp electrophysiology to explain excitability in neurons, muscle, and cardiac tissue. Foundational to pharmacology of channelopathies and neural signaling.
nodes.brief:
Analyzes the biophysical mechanisms of selective ion permeation, gating, and voltage sensing in membrane channels and pumps. Combines electrodiffusion theory, structural biology, and patch-clamp electrophysiology to explain excitability in neurons, muscle, and cardiac tissue. Foundational to pharmacology of channelopathies and neural signaling.

### Concepts
- **Hodgkin-Huxley Formalism** — Models action-potential generation via voltage-dependent sodium and potassium conductances as coupled differential equations.
- **Ion Permeation and Selectivity** — Explains selective ion conduction through channel pores via electrostatic filtering and partial dehydration energetics.
- **Voltage-Sensor Gating** — Describes conformational coupling between transmembrane voltage-sensor domains and pore opening.
- **Patch-Clamp Electrophysiology** — Measures single-channel and whole-cell currents to resolve gating kinetics and conductance states.
- **Gap Junctions and Electrical Coupling** — Characterizes connexon-mediated direct cytoplasmic electrical coupling between adjacent cells.

nodes.name: Single-Molecule & Biophysical Imaging Methods
nodes.en: Develops and applies techniques—optical/magnetic tweezers, single-molecule FRET, AFM, super-resolution microscopy—that resolve the behavior of individual biomolecules beyond ensemble averages. Reveals heterogeneity, rare conformational states, and force-dependent kinetics invisible to bulk assays. Drives much of modern experimental biophysics methodology.
nodes.brief:
Develops and applies techniques—optical/magnetic tweezers, single-molecule FRET, AFM, super-resolution microscopy—that resolve the behavior of individual biomolecules beyond ensemble averages. Reveals heterogeneity, rare conformational states, and force-dependent kinetics invisible to bulk assays. Drives much of modern experimental biophysics methodology.

### Concepts
- **Optical and Magnetic Tweezers** — Apply piconewton-scale forces to single molecules to measure force-extension curves and mechanochemical kinetics.
- **Single-Molecule FRET** — Uses distance-dependent fluorescence energy transfer to track nanometer-scale conformational dynamics in real time.
- **Atomic Force Microscopy** — Probes molecular topography and unfolding forces via cantilever deflection at sub-nanometer resolution.
- **Super-Resolution Fluorescence Microscopy** — Circumvents the diffraction limit via stochastic switching or patterned illumination to localize molecules at tens of nanometers.
- **Single-Particle Tracking** — Follows individual labeled molecules to extract diffusion coefficients and confinement dynamics in living cells.

nodes.name: Mechanobiology & Cellular Mechanics
nodes.en: Investigates how cells generate, sense, and respond to mechanical forces through the cytoskeleton, adhesion complexes, and mechanosensitive signaling. Explains phenomena from tissue morphogenesis to cancer invasion as products of physical force balance. An actively expanding frontier linking cell biology to soft-matter physics.
nodes.brief:
Investigates how cells generate, sense, and respond to mechanical forces through the cytoskeleton, adhesion complexes, and mechanosensitive signaling. Explains phenomena from tissue morphogenesis to cancer invasion as products of physical force balance. An actively expanding frontier linking cell biology to soft-matter physics.

### Concepts
- **Cytoskeletal Rheology** — Characterizes the viscoelastic response of actin, microtubule, and intermediate-filament networks to applied stress.
- **Focal Adhesion Mechanotransduction** — Describes force-dependent conformational changes in talin and integrin complexes that convert mechanical load into signaling.
- **Tissue-Scale Mechanics** — Applies continuum and vertex-model approaches to explain morphogenetic folding, flow, and jamming transitions in epithelia.
- **Mechanosensitive Ion Channels** — Studies pressure- and stretch-gated channels such as Piezo1/2 that transduce membrane tension into electrical signals.
- **Cell Migration Mechanics** — Models actin-driven protrusion, adhesion turnover, and traction-force generation underlying directed cell motility.

nodes.name: Protein Folding & Biomolecular Structure Dynamics
nodes.en: Addresses the physical principles governing how polypeptide chains fold into functional three-dimensional structures and undergo conformational transitions. Uses energy-landscape theory, molecular dynamics simulation, and spectroscopy to explain folding kinetics, misfolding, and allosteric transitions. Central to understanding both normal function and aggregation-linked disease.
nodes.brief:
Addresses the physical principles governing how polypeptide chains fold into functional three-dimensional structures and undergo conformational transitions. Uses energy-landscape theory, molecular dynamics simulation, and spectroscopy to explain folding kinetics, misfolding, and allosteric transitions. Central to understanding both normal function and aggregation-linked disease.

### Concepts
- **Energy Landscape Theory** — Describes folding as diffusion on a funneled free-energy surface biased toward the native conformation.
- **Molecular Dynamics Simulation** — Numerically integrates classical force fields to predict atomistic trajectories of biomolecular motion.
- **Allosteric Conformational Coupling** — Explains how ligand binding at one site propagates structural change to a distant functional site.
- **Protein Misfolding and Aggregation** — Models the kinetics of amyloid nucleation and fibril growth underlying neurodegenerative disease.
- **Intrinsically Disordered Proteins** — Characterizes conformational ensembles of proteins lacking stable tertiary structure yet retaining function.

nodes.name: Nonequilibrium & Active-Matter Biophysics
nodes.en: Treats living systems as intrinsically nonequilibrium matter—continuously dissipating energy to sustain motion, pattern formation, and information processing. Encompasses active-gel theory, stochastic thermodynamics, and collective phenomena in cell populations. Represents the theoretical frontier connecting statistical physics to emergent biological order.
nodes.brief:
Treats living systems as intrinsically nonequilibrium matter—continuously dissipating energy to sustain motion, pattern formation, and information processing. Encompasses active-gel theory, stochastic thermodynamics, and collective phenomena in cell populations. Represents the theoretical frontier connecting statistical physics to emergent biological order.

### Concepts
- **Active Matter Theory** — Models self-propelled agents such as motor-filament mixtures generating spontaneous flow and pattern formation.
- **Stochastic Thermodynamics of Biological Machines** — Quantifies entropy production and efficiency bounds for molecular processes operating far from equilibrium.
- **Collective Cell Dynamics** — Describes flocking, swarming, and jamming transitions in migrating cell populations using active-fluid frameworks.
- **Biological Pattern Formation** — Applies reaction-diffusion and Turing-instability theory to explain self-organized spatial patterning in tissues.
- **Information Thermodynamics in Sensing** — Relates the energetic cost of biochemical sensing accuracy to fundamental thermodynamic bounds.

nodes.name: Botany (Plant Biology)
nodes.en: The biology of plants: their structure and development, photosynthesis and physiology, genetics and genomics, systematics and evolutionary relationships, ecological interactions, and the secondary metabolites they produce. Extends into crop science, the applied study of cultivated plants for agriculture.
nodes.cs: Biologie rostlin: jejich stavba a vývoj, fotosyntéza a fyziologie, genetika a genomika, systematika a evoluční vztahy, ekologické interakce a sekundární metabolity, které produkují. Přesahuje do agronomie — aplikovaného studia pěstovaných rostlin pro zemědělství.

nodes.name: Plant Structure & Development
nodes.en: Studies the organization of plant tissues, organs, and body plans, and the meristematic and hormonal mechanisms that generate them across the life cycle. Covers cell wall biology, vascular architecture, and the developmental genetics governing organ identity and polarity. Foundational to understanding growth, regeneration, and morphological diversity across land plants.
nodes.brief:
Studies the organization of plant tissues, organs, and body plans, and the meristematic and hormonal mechanisms that generate them across the life cycle. Covers cell wall biology, vascular architecture, and the developmental genetics governing organ identity and polarity. Foundational to understanding growth, regeneration, and morphological diversity across land plants.

### Concepts
- **Meristems & Growth** — Apical and lateral meristems maintain stem-cell niches that drive indeterminate plant growth.
- **Vascular Tissue** — Xylem and phloem form the conducting system for water, minerals, and photoassimilates.
- **Cell Wall Biology** — Cellulose, hemicellulose, and pectin networks give plant cells rigidity and control growth anisotropy.
- **Organ Identity Genetics** — ABC(E)-model MADS-box transcription factors combinatorially specify floral organ identity.
- **Root System Architecture** — Lateral root initiation and gravitropic signaling shape the below-ground foraging network.

nodes.name: Photosynthesis & Plant Physiology
nodes.en: Examines the light-dependent and carbon-fixation reactions that convert solar energy into chemical energy, and the whole-plant physiological processes—water transport, gas exchange, hormone signaling—that sustain growth. Encompasses C3, C4, and CAM carbon-concentrating strategies and their adaptive significance. Underpins crop productivity modeling and climate-change response research.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá světelné a karboxylační (Calvinův cyklus) reakce, které přeměňují sluneční energii na chemickou, a fyziologické procesy celé rostliny — transport vody, výměnu plynů, hormonální signalizaci — které udržují její růst. Zahrnuje strategie koncentrace uhlíku C3, C4 a CAM a jejich adaptivní význam. Je základem modelování produktivity plodin a výzkumu reakce na klimatickou změnu.
nodes.brief:
Examines the light-dependent and carbon-fixation reactions that convert solar energy into chemical energy, and the whole-plant physiological processes—water transport, gas exchange, hormone signaling—that sustain growth. Encompasses C3, C4, and CAM carbon-concentrating strategies and their adaptive significance. Underpins crop productivity modeling and climate-change response research.

### Concepts
- **Light Reactions** — Thylakoid photosystems and electron transport chains convert photons into ATP and NADPH.
- **Calvin Cycle & Carbon Fixation** — RuBisCO-driven carboxylation assimilates CO2 into triose phosphates for sugar synthesis.
- **C4/CAM Photosynthesis** — Spatial or temporal CO2-concentrating mechanisms reduce photorespiration in hot or arid climates.
- **Stomatal Physiology** — Guard cell turgor regulates gas exchange and transpirational water loss.
- **Plant Hormone Signaling** — Auxin, gibberellin, cytokinin, ABA, and ethylene networks coordinate growth and stress responses.

nodes.name: Plant Genetics, Genomics & Epigenetics
nodes.en: Investigates the heredity, genome organization, and gene-regulatory mechanisms unique to plants, including polyploidy, transposable-element dynamics, and epigenetic control of development and stress memory. Comparative and functional genomics across model and crop species underlie modern breeding and gene-editing pipelines. A fast-moving field driven by falling sequencing costs and CRISPR-based tools.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá dědičnost, organizaci genomu a genově regulační mechanismy specifické pro rostliny, včetně polyploidie, dynamiky transponovatelných elementů a epigenetické kontroly vývoje a paměti na stres. Srovnávací a funkční genomika napříč modelovými a plodinovými druhy je základem moderních šlechtitelských a genově editačních postupů. Jde o rychle se rozvíjející obor poháněný klesajícími náklady na sekvenování a nástroji založenými na CRISPR.
nodes.brief:
Investigates the heredity, genome organization, and gene-regulatory mechanisms unique to plants, including polyploidy, transposable-element dynamics, and epigenetic control of development and stress memory. Comparative and functional genomics across model and crop species underlie modern breeding and gene-editing pipelines. A fast-moving field driven by falling sequencing costs and CRISPR-based tools.

### Concepts
- **Polyploidy & Genome Duplication** — Whole-genome duplications recurrently reshape plant genome size and gene-family evolution.
- **Transposable Elements** — Mobile genetic elements drive genome plasticity and are epigenetically silenced by RNA-directed DNA methylation.
- **Epigenetic Regulation** — DNA methylation and histone modification establish heritable, environmentally responsive states such as vernalization memory.
- **CRISPR & Genome Editing** — Targeted nuclease systems enable precise trait engineering in crop and model genomes.
- **Quantitative Trait Genetics** — QTL mapping and genome-wide association studies link genotype to agronomic phenotypes.

nodes.name: Plant Systematics & Evolution
nodes.en: Reconstructs phylogenetic relationships among land plants and their algal relatives using morphological and molecular data, and classifies taxa according to evolutionary descent. Traces major innovations—vascular tissue, seeds, flowers—that mark land-plant diversification. Provides the taxonomic backbone for all comparative plant biology.
nodes.cs: Rekonstruuje fylogenetické vztahy mezi suchozemskými rostlinami a jejich řasovými příbuznými pomocí morfologických a molekulárních dat a klasifikuje taxony podle evolučního původu. Sleduje klíčové inovace — cévní pletiva, semena, květy — které vyznačují diverzifikaci suchozemských rostlin. Poskytuje taxonomickou kostru pro veškerou srovnávací biologii rostlin.
nodes.brief:
Reconstructs phylogenetic relationships among land plants and their algal relatives using morphological and molecular data, and classifies taxa according to evolutionary descent. Traces major innovations—vascular tissue, seeds, flowers—that mark land-plant diversification. Provides the taxonomic backbone for all comparative plant biology.

### Concepts
- **Land Plant Phylogeny** — Molecular phylogenetics resolves relationships among bryophytes, ferns, gymnosperms, and angiosperms.
- **Angiosperm Classification** — The APG system organizes flowering-plant families by molecular phylogenetic evidence.
- **Plant Speciation** — Hybridization, polyploid speciation, and reproductive isolation generate new plant lineages.
- **Paleobotany** — Fossil plant evidence documents the evolutionary timeline of vascular tissue, seeds, and flowers.

nodes.name: Plant Ecology & Environmental Interactions
nodes.en: Studies how plants respond to and shape their abiotic and biotic environments, including light competition, nutrient cycling, mycorrhizal symbioses, and herbivore or pathogen defense. Spans population, community, and ecosystem scales and informs conservation and climate-adaptation strategy. Plant-microbiome interactions are a particularly active current frontier.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá, jak rostliny reagují na své abiotické a biotické prostředí a jak ho samy utvářejí, včetně soutěže o světlo, koloběhu živin, mykorhizních symbióz a obrany proti herbivorům či patogenům. Zahrnuje úroveň populací, společenstev i ekosystémů a informuje strategie ochrany přírody a adaptace na klimatickou změnu. Interakce rostlin s mikrobiomem představují obzvláště aktivní současnou výzkumnou frontu.
nodes.brief:
Studies how plants respond to and shape their abiotic and biotic environments, including light competition, nutrient cycling, mycorrhizal symbioses, and herbivore or pathogen defense. Spans population, community, and ecosystem scales and informs conservation and climate-adaptation strategy. Plant-microbiome interactions are a particularly active current frontier.

### Concepts
- **Plant-Microbe Symbiosis** — Mycorrhizal fungi and nitrogen-fixing rhizobia exchange nutrients for plant-derived carbon.
- **Abiotic Stress Response** — Drought, salinity, and temperature stress trigger osmotic adjustment and signaling cascades for acclimation.
- **Plant Defense & Herbivory** — Induced and constitutive defenses deter herbivores and pathogens via chemical and structural barriers.
- **Plant Population & Community Ecology** — Competition, dispersal, and succession structure vegetation community composition over time.
- **Phenology & Climate Response** — Shifting flowering and leaf-out timing track long-term climate-change signals.

nodes.name: Phytochemistry & Secondary Metabolism
nodes.en: Catalogs and explains the ecological and pharmacological roles of plant specialized metabolites—alkaloids, terpenoids, phenolics, and glucosinolates—synthesized outside primary metabolic pathways. These compounds mediate defense, pollinator attraction, and allelopathy, and form the basis of most plant-derived pharmaceuticals. Pathway elucidation increasingly enables metabolic engineering for bioproduction.
nodes.cs: Popisuje a vysvětluje ekologické a farmakologické role specializovaných rostlinných metabolitů — alkaloidů, terpenoidů, fenolů a glukosinolátů — syntetizovaných mimo primární metabolické dráhy. Tyto sloučeniny zprostředkovávají obranu, lákání opylovačů a alelopatii a tvoří základ většiny léčiv rostlinného původu. Objasňování metabolických drah stále více umožňuje metabolické inženýrství pro bioprodukci.
nodes.brief:
Catalogs and explains the ecological and pharmacological roles of plant specialized metabolites—alkaloids, terpenoids, phenolics, and glucosinolates—synthesized outside primary metabolic pathways. These compounds mediate defense, pollinator attraction, and allelopathy, and form the basis of most plant-derived pharmaceuticals. Pathway elucidation increasingly enables metabolic engineering for bioproduction.

### Concepts
- **Terpenoid Biosynthesis** — Mevalonate and MEP pathways generate volatile and defensive terpenoid diversity.
- **Alkaloid Chemistry** — Nitrogen-containing secondary metabolites deter herbivores and underlie many medicinal plant compounds.
- **Phenolic Compounds & Flavonoids** — Phenylpropanoid-derived pigments and antioxidants mediate UV protection and pollinator signaling.
- **Plant Volatile Signaling** — Airborne organic compounds mediate pollinator attraction and inter-plant defense priming.

nodes.name: Crop Science & Applied Botany
nodes.en: Applies botanical principles to cultivated-plant improvement, yield optimization, and sustainable production systems, spanning classical breeding, domestication genetics, and precision agronomy. Addresses global food-security challenges through stress-tolerant cultivar development and resource-efficient cultivation practice. Increasingly integrates genomic selection and phenomics with field-trial data.
nodes.cs: Aplikuje botanické principy na šlechtění kulturních rostlin, optimalizaci výnosu a udržitelné produkční systémy, od klasického šlechtění přes genetiku domestikace po precizní agronomii. Řeší globální výzvy potravinové bezpečnosti vývojem odrůd odolných vůči stresu a zdrojově efektivními pěstitelskými postupy. Stále více propojuje genomovou selekci a fenomiku s daty z polních pokusů.
nodes.brief:
Applies botanical principles to cultivated-plant improvement, yield optimization, and sustainable production systems, spanning classical breeding, domestication genetics, and precision agronomy. Addresses global food-security challenges through stress-tolerant cultivar development and resource-efficient cultivation practice. Increasingly integrates genomic selection and phenomics with field-trial data.

### Concepts
- **Crop Domestication** — Selection for reduced seed shatter and synchronous ripening produced the domestication syndrome in staple crops.
- **Plant Breeding** — Hybridization, marker-assisted selection, and genomic selection accelerate cultivar improvement cycles.
- **Agronomy & Soil-Plant Nutrition** — Fertilization, irrigation, and cropping-system design optimize resource-use efficiency and yield.
- **Post-Harvest Physiology** — Senescence and ripening control determine the storage life and quality of harvested produce.

nodes.name: Zoology (Animal Biology)
nodes.en: Studies the structure, function, behavior, diversity, and evolutionary relationships of animals, spanning comparative anatomy and physiology, ethology, systematics, developmental biology, and physiological adaptation to environmental extremes. Integrates findings across taxa to explain how animal form and behavior arise from and respond to ecological and evolutionary pressures. Distinguished from general physiology and ecology by its taxon-centered organization around the animal kingdom specifically.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá stavbu, funkci, chování, diverzitu a evoluční vztahy živočichů, od srovnávací anatomie a fyziologie přes etologii a systematiku po vývojovou biologii a fyziologickou adaptaci na environmentální extrémy. Propojuje poznatky napříč taxony a vysvětluje, jak tvar těla a chování živočichů vznikají v reakci na ekologické a evoluční tlaky. Od obecné fyziologie a ekologie se odlišuje tím, že je organizována kolem živočišné říše jako taxonomického celku.

nodes.name: Comparative Anatomy & Morphology
nodes.en: Studies the structural organization of animal bodies across taxa, tracing homologous and analogous features to reconstruct evolutionary relationships and functional design. Encompasses gross dissection-based anatomy, histology, and skeletal, muscular, and organ-system comparison across phyla.
